title: Document Intelligence API
description: A fully local, zero-API-cost retrieval-augmented generation pipeline deployed as a 3-service Docker Compose stack.
workflow: **Ingestion Pipeline:** The workflow begins with a user uploading a PDF or TXT file. The document undergoes semantic chunking, and vector embeddings are generated using the `MiniLM-L6-v2` model.
**Vector Storage:** These embeddings are indexed and stored locally in a ChromaDB vector store.

**Retrieval & Generation:** When a user submits a query, the system performs a cosine similarity search against the ChromaDB store to retrieve relevant document context. This context is passed to a local Llama 3.2 model via Ollama to generate an accurate, grounded answer.
**Deployment:** The entire architecture is deployed as a 3-service Docker Compose stack (FastAPI backend, ChromaDB, and Streamlit UI), allowing the system to boot with a single command.

title: ECP Pedestrian Detection
description: A multi-model benchmark and evaluation pipeline built for edge deployment on autonomous vehicles.
workflow: **Data Pipeline:** The workflow starts with the EuroCity Persons (ECP) dataset, containing over 238K instances across 47K images. A custom ECP YOLO annotation converter formats the data for training.
**Model Training:** A YOLOv8s model is fine-tuned on the processed dataset. This fine-tuning raised the mAP@50 metric from 9.1% (SSDLite baseline) to 38.4% (RT-DETR-l).

**Benchmark Interface:** The evaluation workflow utilizes an interactive PySide6 viewer. Through an abstract `BaseAdapter` pattern, 9 different models are hot-swappable via a dropdown menu with zero UI code changes.
**Analysis:** The system performs live detection with FPS and latency overlays rendered via QPainter, and outputs benchmark validation subsets to a CSV export.

title: Spotify Desktop Controller
description: A decoupled desktop application ensuring the PySide6 UI has zero business logic dependencies on the backend.
workflow: **Authentication Flow:** Initiates with an automated local callback server managing a full OAuth 2.0 PKCE flow to securely authenticate with the Spotify API. Tokens are cached locally to bypass re-authentication.
**Backend Communication:** A FastAPI backend handles the Spotipy SDK integration and retry logic seamlessly.
**Frontend Rendering:** The PySide6 frontend communicates with the backend via HTTP. All API polling is handled via `QTimer` to ensure a highly responsive, non-blocking UI thread.
**Custom Graphics:** The visual workflow utilizes a custom rendering pipeline via `QPainter` to handle animated reels and album art, governed by a centralized theme module.
